titleStmt.title: Morale and Home Intelligence Reports
Report: Morale Report for period Dec 44 to Feb 45

div.note: CONFIDENTIAL
HQ ADRIATIC BRIGADE
MORALE REPORT for period Dec 44 to Feb 45 hhh GENERAL
1. The activities of the HQ during the period covered by this report were as follows:-
Dec 1 - Dec 24 - Active ops inside Jugo-slavia
Dec 25 - Jan 30 - (a) Conc in GRUZ area for fresh ops (b) Trg, reorg, sport, entertainment followed by withdrawal to ITALY.
Jan 31 - Feb 28 - Trg, reorg, and leave for all ranks.

2. Morale generally over the period in question has been of a high standard, despite a certain sense of frustration experienced in Jugo-slavia where political influence prejudiced the free conduct of a successful campaign. This was definitely sensed by the men.
3. Morale was assisted by general release of Social Security Plans, Release Scheme and Gratuity Scales. hhh DISCIPLINE
4. Offences against the Army Act committed during the period were as follows:-
AA (Sec) No of Offences Award
Sec 40 1 7 Days CB
Sec 8(2) 1 14 Days Detention

5. A feeling of resentment was apparent towards a discipline drive begun on return from Jugo-slavia, particularly in the case of a HQ guard. This was probably contributed to by reaction after exacting ops, and the general "leave" atmosphere on return to ITALY. hhh CONFIDENCE
6. Confidence is evident in: -
(a) Army leaders and the outcome of the National War Effort.
(b) British arms and eqpt.
(c) General efficiency of all arms. hhh MAIL
7. (a) The removal of the restriction on the issue of Air Letter Cards has been appreciated.

(b) Arrival of mail in Jugo-Slavia was irregular during Dec and early Jan, but improved during the latter part of the period after representation had been made to higher authority. hhh WELFARE AND ENTERTAINMENT
8. (a) Improved during the latter part of Jan with the fmn of a Garrison Canteen, and the arrival in Jugo-Slavia of a Mobile Cinema.

(b) since return to ITALY and to the amenities of BARI, criticism has been made of the control of entertainments. Lack of proper adm results in endless queues for shows, and it is felt that this could be overcome by an allotment plan. Tps have asked for half-days on Weds, as Sats are hopeless from an entertainment point of view.
(c) The lack of sports eqpt and the limited allowance of sports clothing is keenly felt. hhh EDUCATION AND ABCA
9. Has been difficult to org during active ops, but is being arranged during present period of trg.
Tps welcome opportunities to discuss war situation and post-war plans. hhh RELATIONS BETWEEN OFFRS AND MEN
10. Normal relations have obtained during the period. hhh RELATIONS WITH ALLIES

11. Relations with JANL in Jugo-slavia were definitely strained owing to the restrictions imposed on Brit tps by the Partizans; e.g. Brit tps were NOT allowed to visit DUBROVNIK except during certain hrs, and were strongly discouraged from speaking to Jugo-slav civilians at all times.
The persecution by Partizans of local civilians in the DUBROVNIK area was strongly resented by our tps. hhh FINANCE
12. (a) Receipt of AF N 1483 showing tps credit balances is very slow.

(b) The scale of post-war gratuities has been frequently criticized. The majority are apparently of the opinion that all ranks should receive equal recompense for war service irrespective of rank held. hhh LEAVE
13. (a) Leave to ROME has been extremely popular and the amenities of the ROME Rest Camp much appreciated.
(b) The LIAP Scheme gave a big boost to morale on its introduction, although it now appears rather unfortunate that the limitations of the scheme were not made more widely known at the outset.
Maj RM
for Brig
Comd ADRIATIC Bde
CMF
3 Mar 45
